content.problemStatement:
You are given an integer sequence A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1‚Äč,A2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äč. Find the number of integer sequences X1,X2,‚Ä¶,XNX_1, X_2, \ldots, X_NX1‚Äč,X2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,XN‚Äč such that


0‚Č§Xi<2390 \le X_i \lt 2390‚Č§Xi‚Äč<239 for each valid iii
Xi‚Č†XjX_i \neq X_jXi‚ÄčÓÄ†=Xj‚Äč for each valid i‚Č†ji \neq jiÓÄ†=j
(X1‚čÖA1)+(X2‚čÖA2)+‚Ä¶+(XN‚čÖAN)(X_1 \cdot A_1) + (X_2 \cdot A_2) + \ldots + (X_N \cdot A_N)(X1‚Äč‚čÖA1‚Äč)+(X2‚Äč‚čÖA2‚Äč)+‚Ä¶+(XN‚Äč‚čÖAN‚Äč) is divisible by 239239239
Since the answer may be large, compute it modulo 998,244,353998,244,353998,244,353.
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer NNN.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1‚Äč,A2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äč.
content.outputFormat: Print a single line containing one integer ‚Äē the number of sequences satisfying all conditions modulo 998,244,353998,244,353998,244,353.
